query: map vs filter vs reduce javascript interview question

selection.greatFrontendReasonIfOmitted: No exact-topic GreatFrontEnd page was confirmed for this JavaScript prompt, so broader JavaScript question-bank pages would be weaker competitors than topic-direct sources.

competitors.label: MDN Array
competitors.selectionReason: MDN is the canonical reference for array instance methods, including map, filter, and reduce.
theirStrengths: It is authoritative on method signatures, callback behavior, and the full Array API surface.
theirGaps: It is less compact on the interview decision boundary of when each method improves clarity and when reduce is overkill.

realWorldUseCases: These three methods are about transforming arrays without mutating them:
followUpConfusion: map transforms each item, filter keeps some items, and reduce can build any result by accumulating values.

realWorldUseCases: MDN documents the methods thoroughly, but our page is more explicit about the real interview choice between transform, selection, and accumulation.
actionableExamples: The canonical docs still offer strong method examples, so the example criterion stays tied.
followUpConfusion: Our page is clearer on the likely follow-up confusion: reduce is more powerful but often less readable when map or filter already expresses the intent.

actionableExamples: Add one short chained map/filter example later if we need more example separation over the docs.

competitors.winReason: MDN is the canonical array-method reference, but our page is better for this prompt because it centers the decision rule between transform, selection, and accumulation.
